mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that translates into higher corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0 percent quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust employment growth that sustains consumer spending and corporate revenue streams, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of observations when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL index value falls below 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real sales of final goods confirms synchronized macroeconomic expansion that reduces recession probability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5 percent annually, or the combined signal predicts negative returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate profit expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal fails to maintain a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
